scorecard.one_line_pitch: Open-source conversational AI framework for building custom chatbots and voice assistants with machine learning-based NLU and dialogue management
scorecard.wedge: ML-powered conversational AI framework for enterprises needing full control over their chatbot infrastructure, without vendor lock-in to proprietary platforms
scorecard.icp: Enterprise developers and data science teams building customer service bots, internal assistants, or voice applications requiring on-premise deployment and custom training
scorecard.wedge_clarity_reasoning: Clear wedge: an open-source alternative to proprietary chatbot platforms (Dialogflow, Lex) for teams that need full ML control and on-premise deployment. The 'almost 100 languages' claim and 599 contributors signal broad enterprise adoption. However, the README prominently states 'Maintenance Mode' and directs users to 'Hello Rasa' (a new cloud playground), which muddies the current positioning. The original wedge was sharp (Python framework for custom NLU/dialogue), but the transition to CALM/Hello Rasa suggests the company is pivoting away from the OSS roots toward a hosted LLM-based platform. This creates ICP confusion: is it for OSS self-hosters or Hello Rasa cloud users?
scorecard.pull_signals_reasoning: Mixed signals pointing to declining momentum. Strengths: 21,167 GitHub stars (strong cumulative signal), 296K PyPI downloads/month (real usage), 599 contributors (historic community), 3 HN top stories with 100+ points (historic visibility). Critical weaknesses: 0 commits in past 90 days (maintenance mode confirmed), 0 closed issues in 90 days, 0 releases in 90d despite a Jan 2025 release existing. The 1,062 HN mentions in 90d and 325,423 all-time are almost certainly false positives from the common word 'rasa' (Indonesian for 'feeling/taste'). Legitimate HN stories are sparse (3 total, oldest from 2016-2017). The 888K downloads/90d show stickiness from existing users, but with zero development activity, this is maintenance-level pull, not growth. The repo is effectively frozen while the company pivots to Hello Rasa.
scorecard.defensibility_reasoning: Historically had defensibility through ML expertise (custom NLU training, dialogue policies) and a moat of enterprise customers locked into their self-hosted deployments. The 4,909 forks and 599 contributors created ecosystem inertia. However, multiple structural threats: (1) LLMs commoditized NLU overnight—no one wants to train intent classifiers when GPT-4 handles it. (2) The company acknowledges this by pivoting to 'CALM' (LLM + business logic), but this is now a crowded space (Voiceflow, Botpress, OpenAI Assistants API). (3) The OSS repo is in maintenance mode, signaling they're abandoning the original moat. (4) 'Hello Rasa' is a cloud playground, competing with dozens of hosted chatbot builders. (5) No switching costs if the OSS framework is deprecated—customers can migrate to any LLM orchestration framework. The data moat (training data from 599 contributors) is less valuable in an LLM world. The brand has recognition, but defensibility is eroding as they move upmarket into a crowded enterprise SaaS category.
scorecard.bull_case: Rasa has $40M+ in funding (Series B 2020, Series C 2024), 599 OSS contributors, and real enterprise traction (50M+ downloads claimed on homepage, 296K/month on PyPI). The pivot to CALM/Hello Rasa could work if they successfully combine LLM flexibility with deterministic business logic—a real pain point for enterprises that need compliance and auditability. The 'vendor-neutral Agent Engineering Community' positioning is smart: becoming a thought leader/community hub rather than just a framework vendor. If Hello Rasa gains traction as a 'Retool for AI agents,' they could own the enterprise chatbot backend space. The existing customer base (enterprises with self-hosted Rasa deployments) provides a natural upgrade path to Rasa Platform/Hello Rasa. PayPal Ventures and StepStone Group led the Series C, suggesting financial services/enterprise validation.
scorecard.bear_case: The OSS repo—the original wedge—is effectively dead (0 commits/90d), and the company is pivoting to a crowded market (LLM-based chatbot builders). The 21K stars represent past relevance, not future traction. Competitors like LangChain, OpenAI Assistants, and Voiceflow are moving faster with LLM-native architectures. The HN mention count (325K all-time, 1K/90d) is almost entirely false positives from the common Indonesian word 'rasa.' Real HN engagement is minimal (3 stories, none recent). The 'no NLU training' messaging in Hello Rasa undermines the original value prop—why use Rasa when you can just use GPT-4 + LangChain? The maintenance mode announcement signals to the OSS community that Rasa is abandoning them, risking backlash and forks. The 'Agent Engineering Community' is vaporware marketing unless they show real engagement metrics. The enterprise pivot typically means slower growth and longer sales cycles. Without strong differentiation, Hello Rasa is just another YC-style chatbot builder in a saturated market.
scorecard.what_would_change_ranking: Concrete Hello Rasa traction metrics (MAU, enterprise pilots, revenue run-rate) would validate the pivot. Evidence of CALM technical differentiation vs. competitors (published benchmarks, unique IP). Restart of meaningful OSS development (commits, releases) to show dual-track strategy viability. Strong community engagement metrics (forum MAU, Discord activity, not just vanity 'members' count). Public case studies showing enterprises migrating from OSS Rasa to Hello Rasa. Alternatively, if the OSS repo is truly dead, a clear post-mortem explaining the pivot and maintaining goodwill. Third-party validation (analyst reports, enterprise RFPs) positioning Hello Rasa as category leader. Current state: high historic pull, unclear current pull, weak future defensibility.
flags: MAINTENANCE_MODE: README explicitly states 'Rasa Open Source is currently in maintenance mode' with zero commits in 90 days
PIVOT_RISK: Company pivoting from OSS framework to cloud platform (Hello Rasa/CALM), abandoning original wedge
FALSE_POSITIVE_HN: 325K+ HN mentions are almost certainly false positives from common Indonesian word 'rasa'
ZERO_VELOCITY: 0 commits, 0 closed issues, 0 releases in past 90 days despite active repo
CROWDED_MARKET: Hello Rasa competes with LangChain, OpenAI Assistants, Voiceflow, Botpress, and dozens of LLM chatbot builders
COMMUNITY_CHURN_RISK: OSS community may fork or abandon due to maintenance mode announcement
UNCLEAR_MONETIZATION: Transition from OSS to 'Rasa Platform' + 'Rasa-as-a-Service' not clearly explained

data.readme_excerpt:
Rasa Open Source

[![Join the Agent Engineering Community](https://img.shields.io/badge/Community-Join%20the%20Discussion-blueviolet)](https://info.rasa.com/community?utm_source=github&utm_medium=website&utm_campaign=) [![Try Hello Rasa](https://img.shields.io/badge/Playground-Try%20Hello%20Rasa-ff69b4)](https://hello.rasa.ai/?utm_source=github&utm_medium=website&utm_campaign=) [![PyPI version](https://badge.fury.io/py/rasa.svg)](https://badge.fury.io/py/rasa) [![Supported Python Versions](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/rasa.svg)](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/rasa) [![Build Status](https://github.com/RasaHQ/rasa/workflows/Continuous%20Integration/badge.svg)](https://github.com/RasaHQ/rasa/actions) [![Documentation Status](https://img.shields.io/badge/docs-stable-brightgreen.svg)](https://rasa.com/docs)

🚧 Note: Maintenance Mode 🚧

Rasa Open Source is currently in maintenance mode.
The future of building AI agents with Rasa is Hello Rasa and CALM.

## 🚀 The Future of Rasa: Hello Rasa **Building reliable AI agents just got easier.** [**Hello Rasa**](https://hello.rasa.ai/?utm_source=github&utm_medium=website&utm_campaign=) is our new interactive playground for prototyping AI agents. It combines LLM fluency with the reliability of business logic using our **CALM** (Conversational AI with Language Models) engine. ### Why switch to Hello Rasa? * **No setup required:** Open the playground, pick a template (Banking, Telecom, Support), and start building in your browser. * **No NLU training:** We have moved beyond intents. The LLM handles dialogue understanding while you define the business flows. * **Built-in copilot:** A specialized AI assistant helps you generate code, debug flows, and expand your agent instantly. * **Production ready:** Hello Rasa is not just a toy. Export your agent to the Rasa Platform when you are ready to scale. ### Core concepts * **CALM:** Combines LLM flexibility with strict business logic. The LLM understands the user; the code enforces the rules. * **Flows:** Describe logical steps (e.g., collect money, transfer funds) rather than rigid dialogue trees. * **Inspector:** See real-time decision-making. No black boxes. 👉 **[Start building for free at Hello Rasa](https://hello.rasa.ai/?utm_source=github&utm_medium=website&utm_campaign=)** --- ## 🧠 Join the Agent Engineering Community We are building a home for people shipping real-world AI agents. Agent Engineering is evolving faster than any single framework. This is a vendor-neutral space to discuss architectures, memory, orchestration, and safety with builders across the industry. ### What you get: * **Network:** Meet engineers building production agents * **Learn:** Discuss practical patterns, not just theory * **Access:** Direct influence on the Hello Rasa roadmap and early access to features |
data.description: 💬   Open source machine learning framework to automate text- and voice-based conversations: NLU, dialogue management, connect to Slack, Facebook, and more - Create chatbots and voice assistants

data.homepage_text: Skip to content Docs Playground Learn YouTube Blog Community Join the Community How to Contribute Community Showcase Forum Github Get Started Rasa for Enterprises Build ML-powered Conversational AI An open source framework for building text- and voice-based applications. Get started Star Composable Rasa uses a composable set of primitives for natural language understanding and dialogue management, allowing you to build and scale sophisticated conversational AI. Open Source & Flexible Have full control over your machine learning models. Tweak any part of your pipeline, and use the tools you love to analyse model performance. Global & Inclusive Rasa has been used to build bots in almost 100 languages. Build an assistant in your language and share it with our global community. Learn Rasa Join our fast-growing, open source developer community The Rasa Community is a diverse group of developers, data scientists, designers, and conversational AI enthusiasts. 50m + Downloads 15k + Forum Members 750 + Contributors Join the Community Visit the Forum Running Rasa in a large enterprise? Platform for Enterprises World-class, proprietary platform for teams to create transformational conversational customer experiences at enterprise scale. Learn more Enterprise-grade Support Enabling your team throughout the full lifecyle from Proof of Concept to production - with enterprise-grade, service level agreement-based support and an extensive customer success program. Learn more Rasa-as-a-Service With Rasa-as-a-Service, we take care of managing the Rasa Platform so you can move faster. It comes with proactive, premium support and many other benefits like shorter time-to-value. Learn more Join our Newsletter Stay updated with the latest content and news from Rasa around conversational AI!
